locations.description_short: Midsummer Russian field with wildflowers and a resilient crimson Tartar thistle in a ditch.
locations.dramatic_use: Frame narrator's prologue; thistle as metaphor for Hadji Murad's tenacity

locations.description_short: Hostile Chechen mountain aul fifteen miles from Russian lines, saklyas clustered like honeycomb, scented with kizyak smoke.
locations.dramatic_use: Hadji Murad's fugitive refuge where he plots defection; site of his nocturnal escape

aliases: second saklya cut into the hillside
locations.description_short: Clay-plastered whitewashed Chechen house with penthouse, cushions, weapons on nails; hidden hospitality.
locations.dramatic_use: Site of Hadji Murad's secret planning with Sado, dispatch of envoy to Russians, kunak bond

locations.description_short: Village mosque with minarets rising above saklyas; elders assemble here to decide Hadji Murad's fate.
locations.dramatic_use: Offscreen threat locus where villagers resolve to detain Hadji Murad

locations.description_short: Lower village spring where women and children gather at dusk.
locations.dramatic_use: Atmospheric sound-scape of the aul at Hadji Murad's arrival

locations.description_short: Neighboring Chechen aul whose hay was burned by Russians — mentioned, not shown.
locations.dramatic_use: Offscreen marker of Russian reprisals fueling mountaineer grievance

locations.description_short: Shamil's stronghold where a captured Russian soldier was sent — offscreen reference.
locations.dramatic_use: Anchors Shamil's power base geographically

locations.description_short: Second village mentioned as destination for an envoy — brief reference only.
locations.dramatic_use: Node in Hadji Murad's clandestine communication network

aliases: where the river Argun bends by the cliff
locations.description_short: Forest clearing by the Argun river bend with two haystacks and bonfire; hiding place for Hadji Murad's murids.
locations.dramatic_use: Hadji Murad's rendezvous camp before crossing to Russian lines

locations.description_short: Named river whose cliff-bend marks the forest rendezvous.
locations.dramatic_use: Geographic anchor for the secret meeting point

locations.description_short: Advanced Russian military fort ten miles from Makhket; barracks, Shahgirinsk Gate, Vorontsov's quarters within.
locations.dramatic_use: Russian command center; hinge between Chechen wilderness and imperial society

locations.description_short: Gate of Vozdvizhensk Fort through which ambush parties and wood-felling companies depart.
locations.dramatic_use: Threshold from Russian safety into contested forest

locations.description_short: Blackened broken plane tree in night forest, customary outpost site for Russian soldiers.
locations.dramatic_use: Setting for Panov/Avdeev/Nikitin/Bondarenko ambush scene; first Russian foot-soldier POV

locations.description_short: Carpeted, richly curtained drawing room lit by four candles; card table, champagne, Petersburg-level luxury in a Caucasian fort.
locations.dramatic_use: Reveals Vorontsov domestic world; Marya's flirtation with Poltoratsky; envoy's arrival announced

locations.description_short: Commander's residence inside the fort, lit windows long after barracks are dark.
locations.dramatic_use: Seat of Russian aristocratic authority on the frontier

locations.description_short: Small shared officer's cabin inside the fort; Vovilo drunk-asleep at the door, Tikhonov in bed.
locations.dramatic_use: Comic bathetic counterpoint to the drawing-room elegance; shows Poltoratsky's infatuation

locations.description_short: Ravine below the aul with running water; Hadji Murad crosses under pursuit.
locations.dramatic_use: Action-chase beat of Hadji Murad's escape from his pursuers

aliases: where the Russians would be felling trees beyond the Mitchik
locations.description_short: Forest glade beyond the Mitchik river where Russians fell trees; mist, bonfires, sharpshooter line.
locations.dramatic_use: Site of Hadji Murad's formal surrender to Vorontsov and Avdeev's mortal wounding

locations.description_short: River beyond which the wood-cutting expedition and Shalin glade lie.
locations.dramatic_use: Geographic marker delimiting Russian operational territory

locations.description_short: Misty forest road six miles beyond Shahgirinsk Gate, blocked with felled trees and bonfires.
locations.dramatic_use: Staging ground for Poltoratsky's company; approach corridor to surrender site

aliases: bare descending slope of a ravine
locations.description_short: Open ravine between Russian outpost line and young forest where Chechen horsemen appear.
locations.dramatic_use: Tactical space of the skirmish in which Avdeev is shot

locations.description_short: The contested mountain theatre where the whole episode unfolds — Russian empire versus mountaineers.
locations.dramatic_use: Overarching political-geographic frame of the story

locations.description_short: Shamil's sphere of authority under proclamation against harboring Hadji Murad.
locations.dramatic_use: Political territory Hadji Murad dreams of ruling after defeating Shamil

locations.description_short: Hadji Murad's native region, recalled in his ambitions of restored rule.
locations.dramatic_use: Personal-political stake motivating his defection

locations.description_short: Russian imperial capital, invoked as origin of Marya Vasilevna's beauty and the tutor's education.
locations.dramatic_use: Distant metropolis that measures the couple's sense of 'privation' at the fort

notes: Aul name 'Makhmet' in the text is treated as Tolstoy's 'Makhket' (standard English transliteration). 'Vozvizhensk' in ch.2 standardised to 'Vozdvizhensk'. Shalin glade / Mitchik / Argun together form the contested forest corridor — strong candidates for fusion into a single filmic 'border forest' location in a compression pass. Petersburg appears only as biographical reference (not scene) but flagged non-compressible as historical anchor. Vorontsov's Drawing Room and Poltoratsky's Quarters share the same fort but deliberately kept separate — the contrast is the dramatic point. The framing prologue (Countryside Field) is compressible: classic candidate to cut or restage as voiceover over a thistle image. Chekhi and Mitchit Aoul are throwaway name-drops, safe to fuse or cut.
